milestone_brief: Implement roadmap epic realtime-variety-render-e6 -- VTK Flying Edges marching-cubes replacement (CAND-1). Replace skimage.measure.marching_cubes in surfaces.py:_marching_cubes_to_polydata with VTK vtkFlyingEdges3D via pv.ImageData(...).contour([level], method='flying_edges'). SMP-threaded, mathematically identical to MC, zero new deps. Implicit pipeline ONLY (Fermat, Kummer, Enriques x4, Dwork) -- Hanson parametric pipeline untouched (AI-6). Address challenger MINOR objections: (1) Flying Edges drops skimage analytic gradient normals -- derive via compute_normals() post-extraction; add off-screen visual comparison on Kummer + Enriques canonical sextic to confirm no shading regression on high-curvature surfaces; (2) measure real before/after speedup on this machine via the CAND-12 telemetry shipped in e1. Constraints: AI-1, AI-3, AI-6, AI-13, AI-14; tests/test_mesh_generators.py smoke tests guard regressions. Predecessor e1 (CAND-12 telemetry) shipped directly in commit 3e40ddf -- treat the e1 dependency as satisfied.
research_synthesis: Both researchers converged: swap skimage.measure.marching_cubes for pv.ImageData(...).contour([level], method=flying_edges) inside _marching_cubes_to_polydata only. F-order ravel is REQUIRED (C-order silently swaps x/z axes). compute_normals=True gives native gradient normals so the challenger normals-lost objection is moot; the post-Taubin compute_normals() re-derives anyway. origin=(-bounds,)*3 replaces the manual verts-=bounds shift. Keep the zero-crossing ValueError guard before contour (AI-14). Measured ~7-10x MC-step / ~2.5x full-pipeline. Implicit pipeline only; Hanson untouched (AI-6).
